items.q: Is ComplianceAI a substitute for a lawyer?
items.a: No. ComplianceAI's contract analysis, risk scoring, and assistant answers are informational only and do not constitute legal advice. Always consult a licensed lawyer before acting on AI output.

items.q: Is my data stored in Saudi Arabia?
items.a: Yes. Your data is stored on infrastructure located in or accessible from Saudi Arabia and handled in line with the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).

items.q: Which languages does the platform support?
items.a: ComplianceAI is fully bilingual — English and Arabic, including right-to-left layout — across the marketing site and the product.

items.q: Can I cancel my subscription at any time?
items.a: Yes. You can cancel at any time from billing settings; cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period.

items.q: How does the AI cite its sources?
items.a: Every AI-generated analysis, recommendation, or assistant answer includes an inline citation chip referencing the source clause or regulation it's based on.

items.q: Do you offer an Enterprise plan?
items.a: Yes — contact sales for custom seats, SSO, and dedicated support.

checklist.0: Governing law set to Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
checklist.1: Mutual obligations balanced for both parties

nda.intro: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Disclosing Party") and {partyB} ("Receiving Party").
nda.section1Body: The parties wish to explore a potential business relationship and may disclose certain confidential information for that purpose.
nda.section2Body: Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information using no less than a reasonable standard of care and shall not disclose it to third parties.
nda.section3Body: Confidentiality obligations shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure.
nda.section4Body: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes shall be referred to SCCA arbitration in Riyadh.

employment.intro: This Employment Contract ("Contract") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Employer") and {partyB} ("Employee"), in accordance with the Saudi Labor Law.
employment.section1Body: The Employee is engaged in the position agreed between the parties and shall perform the duties assigned by the Employer in good faith.
employment.section2Body: The Employer shall pay the Employee a monthly wage as agreed, payable by the end of each Gregorian month, in addition to statutory benefits (GOSI, end-of-service award).
employment.section3Body: The Employee shall be subject to a probationary period not exceeding ninety (90) days, after which the Contract continues for the term agreed by the parties.
employment.section4Body: This Contract is governed by the Saudi Labor Law and the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes are referred to the competent labor courts.

freelance.intro: This Freelance Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Client") and {partyB} ("Freelancer"), engaged as an independent contractor.
freelance.section1Body: The Freelancer shall perform the services described in the agreed statement of work, exercising independent professional judgment.
freelance.section2Body: The Freelancer is not an employee of the Client and is solely responsible for their own taxes, GOSI (if applicable), and Freelance Certificate compliance.
freelance.section3Body: The Client shall pay the agreed fee upon completion of milestones or on the schedule set out in the statement of work.
freelance.section4Body: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes shall be referred to SCCA arbitration in Riyadh.

vendor.intro: This Vendor Contract ("Contract") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Client") and {partyB} ("Vendor").
vendor.section1Body: The Vendor shall supply the goods and/or services described in the agreed purchase order or statement of work, meeting the specified quality standards.
vendor.section2Body: Pricing is as set out in the agreed schedule; payment is due within the agreed period following delivery and acceptance.
vendor.section3Body: The Vendor shall deliver per the agreed timeline; the Client may inspect and reject non-conforming goods or services within a reasonable period.
vendor.section4Body: This Contract shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes shall be referred to SCCA arbitration in Riyadh.

service.intro: This Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Client") and {partyB} ("Service Provider").
service.section1Body: The Service Provider shall perform the services described in the agreed scope of work to a professional standard.
service.section2Body: The Service Provider shall meet the service levels (SLA) set out in the agreed schedule, including response and resolution times.
service.section3Body: The Client shall pay the agreed fees on the billing schedule set out in this Agreement.
service.section4Body: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes shall be referred to SCCA arbitration in Riyadh.

nonCompete.intro: This Non-Compete Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by and between {partyA} ("Company") and {partyB} ("Employee").
nonCompete.section1Body: The Employee agrees not to engage in any business that directly competes with the Company within the agreed scope.
nonCompete.section2Body: This restriction shall apply for a period not exceeding two (2) years following termination of employment, in line with Saudi Labor Law limits.
nonCompete.section3Body: The restriction applies within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, limited to the markets in which the Company actively operates.
nonCompete.section4Body: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; disputes are referred to the competent Saudi courts.

warningLetter.intro: This letter is issued in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by {partyA} ("Company") to {partyB} ("Employee") regarding the conduct described below.
warningLetter.section1Body: The Employee's conduct on the relevant date did not meet the standards set out in the Company's internal work regulations.
warningLetter.section2Body: This is a formal written warning issued in accordance with the Company's internal disciplinary policy and the Saudi Labor Law.
warningLetter.section3Body: The Employee is required to correct the conduct described above immediately; failure to do so may result in further disciplinary action.
warningLetter.section4Body: This notice is issued in accordance with the Saudi Labor Law and the Company's internal regulations.

terminationLetter.intro: This letter is issued in {city}, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by {partyA} ("Company") to {partyB} ("Employee") to notify the termination of employment.
terminationLetter.section1Body: The Employee's employment with the Company shall end on the agreed termination date, in accordance with the notice period set out in the Contract.
terminationLetter.section2Body: The Employee is entitled to the end-of-service award and any accrued but unused leave, calculated per the Saudi Labor Law.
terminationLetter.section3Body: The Employee shall return all Company property, documents, and access credentials by the termination date.
terminationLetter.section4Body: This notice is issued in accordance with the Saudi Labor Law and the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Review.dropzoneTitle: Drag & drop a contract to analyze
Review.dropzoneSubtitleSuffix: — text is extracted in your browser (PDF, DOCX, TXT); scanned image-only PDFs aren't supported yet

ReviewDetail.askAboutPrompt: I have a question about my document "{filename}":
ReviewDetail.reportDisclaimer: This report is generated by AI for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Always consult a licensed lawyer before acting on it.
ReviewDetail.pendingProcessing: Analysis is running — this can take a few seconds. Refresh in a moment.
ReviewDetail.pendingNotAnalyzed: This document hasn't been analyzed yet. Click "Analyze with AI" from the review list to run it.
ReviewDetail.illustrativeNotice: The risk score, findings, and document viewer below are an illustrative example of what a completed analysis will look like — not real data for this document.
ReviewDetail.noSummary: No summary text was generated for this analysis.
ReviewDetail.noFindingsBody: The AI review completed without flagging any clause-level risks in this document.
ReviewDetail.exampleSummary: This Master Services Agreement carries high overall risk, driven primarily by an uncapped liability clause and a unilateral termination right favoring the supplier. The agreement also lacks a data-protection clause aligned with Saudi PDPL requirements and omits a governing-law provision, which defaults dispute resolution outside the Kingdom. (Example text — not generated for this document.)
ReviewDetail.docTextUnavailableReal: Full document text rendering is not yet implemented — this view will show the document body with inline highlights once available.
ReviewDetail.docTextUnavailableExample: Document text preview is unavailable until the AI pipeline runs. This area illustrates inline clause highlighting once real analysis exists.

indemnity.title: Uncapped client indemnity / unlimited liability
indemnity.note: Clause 9.2 requires the Client to indemnify the Supplier "without limitation," exposing your business to unlimited and consequential damages while the Supplier's liability stays capped at 12 months' fees.
indemnity.recommendation: Make the indemnity mutual and cap aggregate liability for both parties at 12 months' fees. Exclude consequential and indirect damages on both sides.
indemnity.citation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law, Art. 174 · Commercial best practice

termination.title: One-sided termination for convenience
termination.note: The Supplier can terminate on 7 days' notice with no reciprocal right for the Client, creating service-continuity and switching risk.
termination.recommendation: Grant both parties symmetric termination rights with a minimum 30–60 day notice period and a transition-assistance obligation.

ipOwnership.title: IP ownership tied to payment milestones
ipOwnership.note: Deliverables remain Supplier property until "full and final payment," leaving ownership ambiguous during disputes or staged payments.
ipOwnership.recommendation: Define IP assignment on a per-milestone basis and grant an interim licence to avoid work stoppage.
ipOwnership.citation: Saudi Copyright Law · IP best practice

governingLaw.title: Governing law not specified
governingLaw.note: The jurisdiction placeholder is unfilled. For a Saudi entity, disputes should be resolved under KSA law.
governingLaw.recommendation: Specify the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Riyadh courts / SCCA arbitration.

dataProtection.title: No personal data protection clause
dataProtection.note: The contract involves processing of customer personal data but contains no PDPL-compliant data processing terms, retention limits, or breach notification obligations.
dataProtection.recommendation: Add a data processing addendum specifying lawful basis, retention period, and breach notification timelines consistent with the PDPL.

StayCompliant.remindersDesc: Email + dashboard alerts are sent via the notify_emails list on each event.
StayCompliant.errorCreate: Failed to save the deadline. Please try again.
StayCompliant.fieldCategoryPlaceholder: e.g. ZATCA VAT return, CMA disclosure
StayCompliant.emailConfigDesc: Connect your own mail server so reminder emails are sent from your domain. Your password is encrypted before it's stored and never shown again.
StayCompliant.emailTestHint: Save your configuration first to send a test.

sections.title: 1. Information we collect
sections.body: We collect account information (name, email, organization), documents you upload for review or generation, and usage data needed to operate the service (logs, device/browser metadata).

sections.title: 2. How we use your information
sections.body: We use your data to provide contract review, document generation, compliance tracking and the AI assistant; to maintain and secure the platform; and to communicate with you about your account.

sections.title: 3. AI processing
sections.body: Documents and questions you submit may be processed by third-party AI providers to generate analysis, drafts, or answers. AI output is informational only and does not constitute legal advice.

sections.title: 4. Data storage and retention
sections.body: Your data is stored on infrastructure located in or accessible from Saudi Arabia and retained for as long as your account is active, or as required by applicable law, including the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).

sections.title: 5. Data sharing
sections.body: We do not sell your data. We share data only with service providers that help us operate the platform (e.g. hosting, AI processing), under contractual confidentiality obligations, or when required by law.

sections.title: 6. Your rights
sections.body: Under the PDPL, you may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data, and may withdraw consent to processing where applicable. Contact us to exercise these rights.

sections.title: 7. Security
sections.body: We apply industry-standard technical and organizational measures, including encryption in transit and access controls, to protect your data against unauthorized access, loss, or misuse.

sections.title: 8. Contact
sections.body: For questions about this policy or to exercise your data rights, contact us at privacy@complianceai.sa.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
sections.body: By accessing or using ComplianceAI, you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

sections.title: 2. Description of service
sections.body: ComplianceAI provides AI-assisted contract review, document generation, compliance deadline tracking and a legal assistant. The service is informational and does not constitute legal advice.

sections.title: 3. Not legal advice
sections.body: AI-generated analysis, recommendations, drafts and chat responses are provided for informational purposes only. You should consult a licensed lawyer before relying on any output for a legal decision.

sections.title: 4. Accounts and organizations
sections.body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activity under your organization. You must provide accurate information when registering.

sections.title: 5. Acceptable use
sections.body: You agree not to use the service to upload unlawful content, infringe third-party rights, attempt to reverse-engineer the platform, or interfere with its security or availability.

sections.title: 6. Subscriptions and billing
sections.body: Paid plans are billed in advance on a recurring basis as described at checkout. You may cancel at any time; cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.

sections.title: 7. Intellectual property
sections.body: The platform, its design and underlying technology are owned by ComplianceAI. Documents you upload or generate remain your property; you grant us a limited license to process them to provide the service.

sections.title: 8. Limitation of liability
sections.body: ComplianceAI is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from use of the service.

sections.title: 9. Termination
sections.body: We may suspend or terminate access to the service for violation of these terms. You may stop using the service and close your account at any time.

sections.title: 10. Governing law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent Saudi courts.

sections.title: 11. Changes to these terms
sections.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the service after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

sections.title: 12. Contact
sections.body: For questions about these Terms and Conditions, contact us at legal@complianceai.sa.
